seoTitle: How to Create a Template in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to create reusable email templates in Freshdesk to reply to common questions faster and more consistently.
intro: Templates save your team from retyping the same answers to common questions. This guide walks through creating a reusable template in Freshdesk.

steps.heading: Open the templates section
steps.body: In your admin settings, find the area for managing email templates or canned responses.

steps.heading: Start a new template
steps.body: Create a new template and give it a descriptive name so agents can find it easily later.

steps.heading: Write the template content
steps.body: Draft the reply text, using placeholders for dynamic fields like the customer's name where supported.

steps.heading: Save and make it available
steps.body: Save the template so it appears as an option agents can insert when replying to tickets.

faq.question: Can templates include dynamic fields?
faq.answer: Yes, Freshdesk templates typically support placeholders that pull in details like the customer name or ticket number automatically.

faq.question: Who can create templates?
faq.answer: Template creation is usually available to admins, though some plans allow agents to create their own as well.

faq.question: Can I organize templates into folders or categories?
faq.answer: Yes, Freshdesk often allows you to group templates for easier browsing when there are many of them.

faq.question: Can templates be edited after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back and update a template's wording at any time.
